title: DLRS Hosted-API Opt-In Policy
description: Declarative per-record policy that explicitly opts into running hosted (online) AI APIs against a record's data. Default DLRS pipelines are offline-first; this document is the *only* mechanism by which a hosted-API code path may execute. Lives at <record>/policy/hosted_api.json. See pipelines/_hosted_api.py for the runtime gate.

schema_version.description: Schema version pin. Bump in lock-step with this file's $id when breaking changes happen.

opt_in.description: Master switch. MUST be set to true for any hosted-API code path to be permitted; setting it to false short-circuits every other field. Absence of this file is treated identically to opt_in=false.

allowed_providers.description: Whitelist of provider identifiers permitted under this policy. The runtime gate checks `provider in allowed_providers`; an empty list denies everything regardless of opt_in.

allowed_pipelines.description: Whitelist of pipeline names (matching pipelines.PipelineSpec.name) that may invoke hosted APIs under this policy. A pipeline NOT in this list MUST refuse to run any hosted-API code path even if other fields would permit it.

consent_evidence_ref.description: Pointer to the consent artefact that authorises hosted-API processing for this record. MUST resolve inside the record (relative path) or be an opaque ticket id documented in audit/events.jsonl. Examples: 'consent/hosted_api_consent.pointer.json', 'consent/hosted_api_consent.video.pointer.json'.

issued_at.description: ISO-8601 UTC timestamp at which the data subject (or their authorised agent) issued this opt-in.

expires_at.description: ISO-8601 UTC timestamp after which this opt-in MUST be re-issued. The runtime gate refuses to authorise hosted-API calls past this point. Setting expires_at <= issued_at is invalid.

data_residency.description: Optional human-readable note about which region the hosted provider processes data in (e.g. 'EU only', 'US-East'). Free-form; consumers SHOULD log it but MUST NOT branch policy on it.

rate_limits.description: Optional, additive per-provider rate guidance. Schema-validated content goes under named subkeys; this is for additive context.

notes.description: Free-form contextual note (e.g. 'reviewed by legal 2026-04-01'). Sensitive identifiers MUST NOT be embedded here.
